node.title: Uber | OA 2020 | Implement Increment Keys function for hashmap

topic.title: Uber | OA 2020 | Implement Increment Keys function for hashmap

post.content:
I was asked this question recently on uber online assessment. 
1) Implement Increment function for all values in hashmap
2) Implement Increment function for all keys in hashmap

First task  is easy. a) get a keyset  b) for every key in key set, increment its value
Second task is not so trivial. 
For every key, keep checking if there is key which is equal to key +1 and keep going until there is no key which is equal to key + 1. increment all the keys in reverse order. 

I would like to know if there is an efficient way to implement 2.

Maybe having a sorted descending Map or a ```TreeMap```  in the second case would be helpful. The sorted map can be traversed and incremented and you are always guaranteed the key above it will be greater than it by 1 and the key below it will be less by 1. So incrementing it will not require us to check for if key + 1 exists. I don\'t know exactly know if it works. Could try implementing it if I had a valid test case as we are modifying the keys I would like to know-how is the caller checking for validity of our solution?
is time complexity O(1) for each operation?
Was anyone able to solve this problem?\n
Just to make sure. Does it request o(1) additional space?
Which location is this ?\nWhat is ur total exp?
